commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: A maintainer should decide the doctor exec-consent policy before this security-sensitive behavior change merges.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete command-execution policy concern by resolving exec SecretRefs during default doctor gateway-auth checks.; Review finding: [P1] Do not execute exec SecretRefs during default doctor checks — src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts:122-127 (sha=12d31f44f9e309d0676245417f771bec34aa39c8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes doctor gateway-auth checks to resolve `gateway.auth.token` through the SecretRef-aware gateway token resolver before warning, and adds an e2e regression case for a resolved env SecretRef.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows a SecretRef-backed `gateway.auth.token` is not materialized by `resolveGatewayAuth()`, so the doctor gateway-auth check reaches the configured-SecretRef warning path; I did not run the command because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: A maintainer should decide the doctor exec-consent policy before this security-sensitive behavior change merges.

target.title: fix(doctor): resolve gateway SecretRef in auth checks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw doctor --lint --json` can report `core/doctor/gateway-auth` as unavailable when `gateway.auth.token` is a SecretRef that resolves successfully.
- Solution: resolve the configured gateway token through the existing SecretRef-aware resolver before emitting the gateway-auth warning.
- What changed: both the lint/core health check and interactive doctor health path now suppress the warning when the SecretRef resolves, and include the resolver's concrete failure reason when it does not.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change gateway startup auth, SecretRef provider behavior, service install behavior, or token storage.

AI-assisted: prepared with Codex.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77687
- Related #65201
- Related #77698
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

- A real OpenClaw setup had a healthy SecretRef-backed gateway token, but doctor lint still reported `core/doctor/gateway-auth` as unavailable. That warning points users toward unnecessary secret rotation or replacing SecretRef-backed config with plaintext.
- #77698 fixes the same general interactive doctor warning direction, but the local observed failure was from the lint/core health check path and needs equivalent handling in `doctor-core-checks.ts`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: false `core/doctor/gateway-auth` warning for a successfully resolving SecretRef-backed `gateway.auth.token`.
- Real environment tested: macOS, local OpenClaw source checkout used by the CLI and gateway service, `gateway.auth.mode: "token"`, `gateway.auth.token` stored as an `exec` SecretRef via a local 1Password CLI wrapper. Secret values, item paths, account identifiers, vault names, host-specific paths, and bootstrap credentials are intentionally omitted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/doctor.warns-state-directory-is-missing.e2e.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/doctor-gateway-auth-token.test.ts`
  - `pnpm build`
  - `openclaw doctor --lint --json`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```shell
pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/doctor.warns-state-directory-is-missing.e2e.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       13 passed (13)

pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/doctor-gateway-auth-token.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       13 passed (13)

pnpm build
# passed

openclaw doctor --lint --json
# no core/doctor/gateway-auth finding
# remaining finding was unrelated Claude CLI auth expiry
```

- Observed result after fix: doctor lint no longer reports `core/doctor/gateway-auth` when the configured gateway token SecretRef resolves.
- What was not tested: a live failing SecretRef provider path; the unresolved branch is covered by existing doctor behavior and now preserves the warning while adding the resolver's detailed reason.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```json
{
  "checkId": "core/doctor/gateway-auth",
  "severity": "warning",
  "message": "Gateway token is managed via SecretRef and is currently unavailable.",
  "path": "gateway.auth.token"
}
```

The same setup's SecretRef-aware token resolver returned this redacted result before the patch, without printing the token:

```json
{
  "hasToken": true,
  "source": "secretRef",
  "secretRefConfigured": true
}
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: doctor checked gateway auth through `resolveGatewayAuth()`, which intentionally does not materialize SecretRef object values. For SecretRef-backed token auth, that left `auth.token` unset and made doctor report the configured token as unavailable.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing doctor coverage asserted the unresolved SecretRef warning branch, but did not cover a resolved SecretRef no-warning case.
- Contributing context (if known): #77698 identified the same interactive doctor warning path. This PR extends the fix shape to the lint/core health check path as well.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/doctor.warns-state-directory-is-missing.e2e.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a local token-auth gateway config with `gateway.auth.token` as an env SecretRef should not emit the Gateway auth SecretRef warning when the SecretRef resolves.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the existing doctor command flow around gateway auth notes without needing a live external secret provider.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/commands/doctor-gateway-auth-token.test.ts` already covers token resolver behavior; the new test covers doctor behavior.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Doctor no longer warns that a SecretRef-managed gateway token is unavailable when that SecretRef resolves.
- If the SecretRef does not resolve, doctor still warns but now includes the resolver's concrete failure reason when available.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
gateway.auth.token SecretRef -> resolveGatewayAuth() -> no materialized token -> false unavailable warning

After:
gateway.auth.token SecretRef -> resolveGatewayAuthToken() -> resolved token -> no warning
gateway.auth.token SecretRef -> resolveGatewayAuthToken() -> unresolved -> warning with reason
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - The change resolves the already-configured `gateway.auth.token` SecretRef through the existing gateway token resolver, after doctor has determined token auth needs a token. It does not print the token or persist it into config. The behavior matches existing runtime/service audit resolver usage.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout / Node CLI
- Model/provider: not model-dependent
- Integration/channel (if any): local gateway auth / doctor CLI
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` and `gateway.auth.token` as a SecretRef.

### Steps

1. Configure local gateway token auth with `gateway.auth.token` as a resolving SecretRef.
2. Run `openclaw doctor --lint --json` on current main.
3. Observe `core/doctor/gateway-auth` warning.
4. Apply this patch and rebuild.
5. Run `openclaw doctor --lint --json` again.

### Expected

- A resolving SecretRef-backed gateway token should not produce an unavailable-token warning.

### Actual

- After this patch, the warning is absent for the tested resolving SecretRef setup.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Resolved SecretRef-backed gateway token no longer emits `core/doctor/gateway-auth`.
  - Existing unresolved SecretRef read-only doctor behavior remains covered.
  - Gateway token resolver tests still pass.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Lint/JSON doctor path and interactive doctor path both use the SecretRef-aware resolver before warning.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - A live unresolved external SecretRef provider failure path.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: doctor resolves an `exec` SecretRef during the gateway auth check.
  - Mitigation: this uses the existing `resolveGatewayAuthToken()` path, does not print or persist the token, and only runs after doctor determines local token auth needs a materialized token. This preserves support for setups where `exec` SecretRefs are the configured gateway credential source.
target.author: funmerlin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
